label: Dynamical systems & control theory

node.label: Dynamical systems & control theory
node.definition: State, stability, observability, feedback, and optimal control govern systems that evolve and act over time.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Dynamical systems & control theory is to connect Differential equations, Linear algebra to downstream capabilities including Neuroscience & cognitive science, Markov chains, MDPs, POMDPs & belief states, Multimodal, simulated & streaming data.
examples: substrate for Markov chains, MDPs, POMDPs & belief states
substrate for Multimodal, simulated & streaming data
limitations: Its abstractions require explicit assumptions; a mathematically valid result can still be irrelevant to a mismatched empirical setting. Its direct dependencies include Differential equations, Linear algebra.

dependencies.rationale: Dynamical systems & control theory rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Differential equations.
Dynamical systems & control theory rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Linear algebra.
Dynamical systems & control theory rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Optimization theory.

sentence_units.text: State, stability, observability, feedback, and optimal control govern systems that evolve and act over time.
